authoritative_sources.title: Child and Adult Care Food Program
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Agriculture Food and Nutrition Service
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USDA FNS is the federal authority for CACFP and this official page defines participating settings, reimbursement posture, and state-agency administration for child-care nutrition workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: baseline program participation and reimbursement hierarchy review

authoritative_sources.title: Nutrition Standards for CACFP Meals and Snacks
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Agriculture Food and Nutrition Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official USDA technical-assistance page names the meal-pattern rules, meal and snack tables, infant-feeding references, and Food Buying Guide dependencies that directly govern child-care nutrition coordination.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: meal-service dependency validation and compliant substitution routing

authoritative_sources.title: A Quick Guide to CACFP Five-Day Reconciliation
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Agriculture Food and Nutrition Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official USDA workflow guide explains how meal counts, attendance, and enrollment must reconcile in participating centers, which makes it authoritative for claim-integrity blockers and refusal triggers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: record reconciliation before sponsor or state handoff

authoritative_sources.title: Requirement to Accept Medical Statements from Registered Dietitians for Meal Accommodations in the School Meal Programs and Child and Adult Care Food Program
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Agriculture Food and Nutrition Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This USDA policy memo is authoritative for which practitioners may provide meal-accommodation medical statements, directly constraining evidence acceptance and routing.

authoritative_sources.title: Q&As: Milk Substitution for Children with Medical or Special Dietary Needs (Non-Disability)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Agriculture Food and Nutrition Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official USDA Q&A clarifies when milk substitutions may be supported for non-disability dietary needs and what documentation is required, making it directly relevant to coordination decisions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: special-diet routing and family or health handoff preparation
used_for: family preference versus documented need boundary

authoritative_sources.title: 1302.44 Child nutrition.
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This Head Start regulation is authoritative for child nutrition services, daily meal coverage, infant feeding, breastfeeding support, water access, and USDA payment-source precedence in Head Start programs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: program-model-specific nutrition routing and funding hierarchy checks

authoritative_sources.title: Promoting Healthy Eating and Nutrition for Head Start Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This Head Start implementation memorandum is authoritative for operationalizing nutrition services, breastfeeding support, family engagement, and alignment between Head Start and CACFP expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cross-team coordination and family-facing nutrition routing

authoritative_sources.title: Caring for Children with Food Allergies
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official Head Start guidance names care-plan, staff-training, emergency-response, and family-notification requirements for food-allergy workflows in early-childhood settings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: allergy-related blocker escalation and adjacent-lane routing

authoritative_sources.title: Caring for Our Children
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Resource Center for Health and Safety in Child Care and Early Education
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFOC is a recognized national standards set for early care and education and is appropriate as a secondary workflow reference for nutrition, food service, and allergy-safety terminology after federal sources are established.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: terminology tightening and secondary safety framing

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and nutrition prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate program participation, attendance, enrollment, meal counts, menu or substitution records, accommodation evidence, and child-specific feeding context before ownership transfer.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team meal-service and dietary-accommodation routing
workflow_stages.description: Coordinate which team owns kitchen execution, classroom communication, health review, sponsor response, family outreach, or approval next.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Blocker escalation and authority conflict handling
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when owner ambiguity, missing accommodation evidence, conflicting policy, or potential overclaim exposure prevents safe progression.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and reimbursement-safe transfer
workflow_stages.description: Confirm receiving owner, service date, remaining blockers, family communication state, and claim-impact notes are explicit before a case closes.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Program participation and sponsor relationship records
Daily meal counts by meal type and service date
Menus, meal-pattern worksheets, production records, and substitution logs
Food allergy care plans, medical statements, milk substitution forms, and infant feeding instructions
Breast milk or formula handling notes and service logs
Monitoring findings, reconciliation notes, corrective-action or overclaim references
Family communication notes, sponsor response drafts, handoff summaries, blocker logs, routing confirmations, and closure confirmations
systems_of_record: CACFP claiming system or sponsor portal
Menu planning, purchasing, or kitchen production systems
Child health record, allergy plan, or special-diet repository
Task queue or workflow routing system

allowed: Validate child-nutrition-specific prerequisites and evidence completeness before routing work.
Track dependency gaps, ownership conflicts, and stale handoffs across classroom, kitchen, sponsor, family-services, and health teams.
Produce evidence-backed routing, blocker, and closure summaries without overstating approval authority.
Escalate when a nutrition request crosses into fiscal, clinical, food-safety, licensing, legal, or policy-interpretation authority.
escalate: Missing program participation status, sponsor context, meal count basis, medical statement, care plan, or infant feeding record.
Conflicting USDA, Head Start, sponsor, jurisdiction, or tenant rules on meal service, accommodations, breastfeeding, or claim handling.
Possible allergic reaction, food-safety incident, overclaim exposure, or family-impacting escalation.
refuse: Direct claim approval, budget or procurement approval, or reimbursement policy exception requests.
Clinical or emergency treatment judgment, diagnosis, or final dietary prescription.
Any routing action that would place a child into meal service without the required accommodation, care-plan, or feeding evidence.

domain_failure_modes: Meal-service handoff without attendance, enrollment, or meal-count records can create unsupported reimbursement claims or monitoring failures.
A menu accommodation routed without a medical statement or food-allergy care plan can expose children to unsafe feeding conditions and improper documentation.
Conflicting ownership between classroom, kitchen, sponsor, and family-services teams can drop nutrition work unless arbitration is explicit.
Generic office-food logic can wrongly treat child-care meal service as simple catering and ignore meal patterns, infant feeding, and child-specific accommodations.
Head Start, CACFP, sponsor, and tenant requirements can materially differ, so forcing a single answer without program-model context creates false confidence.
Infant feeding workflows differ from preschool meal service because bottle handling, breast milk storage, formula instructions, and on-demand feeding requirements change the handoff.
Not every child-care site participates in CACFP, so assuming reimbursement workflows exist everywhere overgeneralizes from the industry label.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams
A handoff is attempted without the required context

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State, tribal, territorial, and local food-safety or sanitation rules
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: These rules can change kitchen, permit, storage, transport, and classroom food-service requirements.
unresolved_ambiguities.why_not_blocking: The pack treats them as required tenant or jurisdiction retrieval facts and refuses to synthesize a final answer when they are missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Sponsor-specific forms, reconciliation cadence, and claim-adjustment workflows
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: CACFP sponsors and state agencies may require different evidence bundles or approval sequencing.
unresolved_ambiguities.why_not_blocking: The public USDA baseline is sufficient for validated coordination rules, while sponsor-specific differences are modeled as runtime retrieval dependencies.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Variation across Head Start, CACFP-participating centers, family child care homes, and non-CACFP sites
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Program model changes funding, meal-service, and record expectations.
unresolved_ambiguities.why_not_blocking: The pack requires program-model identification before routing and includes explicit refusal or escalation when that fact is missing.

summary: The public USDA and Head Start baseline is sufficient to define child-care-nutrition-specific workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, and failure modes for a validated coordination spec pack. Remaining variability is handled through required runtime retrieval and explicit escalation rather than unsupported synthesis.
